chain.thought: Explanation: The entrant column shows "private entry" in rows 4, 5, 7, 10, 12, 13, 16, 17, and 18. Counting these gives a total of 9 private entries.

judge_thought:
Explanation:
The table uses two different ways to denote privately entered cars: some rows explicitly label the entrant as “private entry” (e.g., Pietro Nicolotti, Fritz Caflisch, Arrigo Sartorio, Heinrich Joachim von Morgen, Emil Frankl, Cesare Renzi, Cesare Pastore, Colonna de Stigliano, Cleto Nenzioni), while others list individual names as entrants (e.g., “Enrico or F. Sartorio,” “A. or E. Nenzioni) without the “private entry” tag. Because it is unclear whether to count only those explicitly labeled “private entry” or also include the individually named entrants—which are likely private as well—the exact number of private entries cannot be determined unambiguously from the table alone. Therefore, returning “null” appropriately reflects the indeterminate count.
